---
title: |
  <img src="../_assets/ahri_logo.png" alt="AHRI logo" class="title-logo" />
  <br />
  CO-LUMINATE Psychometric Harmonization
subtitle: "Preliminary SSQ-PHQ Comparability Findings"
author:
  - name: CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
    affiliation: Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)
    city: Durban & Somokhele
    country: South Africa
format:
  revealjs:
    controls: true
    progress: true
    slide-number: true
    css:
      - styles/presentation_0.css
      - styles/presentation_1.css
---

## Scope

- Objective: establish comparability between SSQ and PHQ depression measures in the CO-LUMINATE psychometric stream.
- Outputs summarize structure stability, calibration performance, and score harmonization evidence.

## Analytical Workflow

1. Monte Carlo EFA for factor-structure stability.
2. Monte Carlo CFA for model-fit and invariance evaluation.
3. IRT plus ROC calibration for harmonized outcome interpretation.
4. Integration of diagnostics into analysis-ready guidance.

## Factor Retention and Structural Evidence

![](/_assets/obj03/fig-factor-retention-1.png){width=92%}

## Core Psychometric Findings

![](/_assets/obj03/fig-figure1-1.png){width=95%}

## Calibration and Validity

![](/_assets/obj03/fig-irtroc-1.png){width=92%}

![](/_assets/obj03/fig-ssq08-validity-1.png){width=92%}

## Pattern Diagnostics

![](/_assets/obj03/fig-heatmap-ssq10-pa-1.png){width=90%}

## Implications for CO-LUMINATE

- Preliminary evidence supports harmonized use of SSQ and PHQ constructs for depression-outcome derivation.
- Psychometric comparability enables more coherent downstream modeling across linked datasets.
- Next step: consolidate finalized psychometric outputs into the integrated project analysis pipeline.
